questions.question_text: A Mississippi salesperson places a newspaper ad featuring only the property address and her personal phone number. What violation has occurred?
questions.correct_answer: The ad lacks the broker's name and phone
distractors: The ad should include the MLS number
No violation if the salesperson is licensed
The ad needs the property's legal description
questions.explanation: Mississippi requires all advertising to be in the name of the broker or firm, prominently displayed, and to include the broker's or firm's telephone number. An ad showing only a street address and a phone number is a prohibited blind ad under MREC Rule 3.3.

questions.question_text: Under MREC rules, who must directly supervise all advertising produced by affiliated licensees?
distractors: The MREC advertising committee
The individual licensee's team leader
The Mississippi Secretary of State
questions.explanation: All advertising must be under the direct supervision and in the name of the principal (responsible) broker or the brokerage firm. Advertising published outside the broker's direct supervision violates MREC Rule 3.3.

questions.question_text: A broker reviews an ad draft that names a salesperson who recently transferred to another firm. What should the broker do?
distractors: Publish if the salesperson consents
Add a disclaimer about the transfer
File a cooperative agreement with MREC
questions.explanation: Every person named in an advertisement must be an active licensee affiliated with the advertising broker. Since the salesperson transferred, naming her would violate MREC advertising rules.

questions.question_text: Which two elements must appear in every Mississippi real estate advertisement?
questions.correct_answer: Broker or firm name and phone number
distractors: Agent name and license number
MLS number and listing price
Property address and broker email
questions.explanation: MREC Rule 3.3 requires every advertisement to prominently display the broker or firm name and to include the telephone number of the principal broker or brokerage firm. This ensures consumers can identify the responsible brokerage.

questions.question_text: A salesperson creates a flyer without the broker's prior knowledge. What is the most likely MREC disciplinary consequence for the broker?
distractors: A verbal warning only
A mandatory continuing education course
No consequence for the broker
questions.explanation: All advertising must be under the broker's direct supervision. Failing to abide by MREC rules is prima facie improper dealing, and improper dealing and misleading advertising are grounds for suspension or revocation under Miss. Code Ann. §73-35-21.

questions.question_text: A salesperson posts a property listing on her personal Instagram page with only her name. Under MREC rules, what is missing?
distractors: Her individual license number
The official MREC logo
A fair housing disclaimer
questions.explanation: Social media postings are classified as advertising under MREC rules. The same requirements apply: the broker or firm name and telephone number must be prominently displayed.

questions.question_text: Under MREC rules, how are social media posts by licensees classified?
questions.correct_answer: As advertising subject to all ad rules
distractors: As personal expression outside regulation
As marketing exempt from broker approval
As informal communication with no rules
questions.explanation: MREC classifies all social media and internet postings by licensees about real estate services as advertising. The same rules that apply to traditional advertising apply to online postings.

questions.question_text: A licensee shares a listing on Facebook with the broker's name but no phone number. Is this compliant?
questions.correct_answer: No, the phone number is also required
distractors: Yes, the broker name alone is sufficient
Yes, phone numbers are optional online
Only if the post links to the firm website
questions.explanation: Both the broker or firm name AND telephone number must appear in all advertising, including social media posts. Omitting the phone number violates MREC advertising rules.

questions.question_text: MREC discovers a licensee's real estate blog contains multiple posts without broker identification. What action may MREC take?
distractors: Issue a fine but take no other action
Require the licensee to delete the blog
Refer the case to federal regulators
questions.explanation: Advertising violations, including internet and social media noncompliance, are grounds for suspension or revocation of a license under MREC rules.

questions.question_text: A licensee shares a listing on a third-party website. What must the listing include under MREC advertising rules?
distractors: Only the agent's personal cell number
Only the property's MLS listing ID
Only a link to the brokerage website
questions.explanation: All advertising, including listings on third-party websites, must include the broker or firm name and telephone number. This requirement applies to every platform where a licensee advertises real estate services.
